label: Uncomplicated ankle bruise without fracture or neurovascular red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated ankle bruise/contusion.
No fracture/dislocation concern, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, inability to bear weight, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Fracture/dislocation concern, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, severe pain, inability to bear weight, high-energy trauma, crush injury, compartment syndrome concern, anticoagulation/bleeding risk, or unexplained bruising/abuse concern.
Pregnancy/pediatric pathway or specialist-directed orthopedic plan.
must_not_miss: Bleeding disorder or unsafe bruising pattern.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, or orthopedics if pain, swelling, or walking is not improving.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports bruise/contusion framing, local care concepts, and escalation for severe pain, neurovascular symptoms, fracture concern, bleeding risk, or unusual bruising. Autonomous Phase 2101-2200 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated ankle bruise/contusion without fracture/dislocation concern, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, inability to bear weight, high-risk trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. no fracture/dislocation concern, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, high-risk trauma, or specialist-directed plan documented
